text:
=F7 =D0=CE, 04/09/2006 =D7 10:05 -0400, michael johnson =D0=C9=DB=C5=D4:
>=20
>=20
> On 9/4/06, Vladimir Grebenschikov <vova@fbsd.ru> wrote:
>        =20
>         >Number:         102843
>         >Category:       ports
>         >Synopsis:
>         graphics/gqview-devel: /libexec/ld-elf.so.1: /usr/local/lib/libgt=
hread-2.0.so.0: Undefined symbol "pthread_getschedparam"
>         >Confidential:   no=20
>         >Severity:       serious
>         >Priority:       medium
>         >Responsible:    freebsd-ports-bugs
>         >State:          open
>         >Quarter:

>         >Environment:
>        =20
>        =20
>         System: FreeBSD 7.0-CURRENT #8: Sat Sep  2 01:16:05 MSD 2006
>             root@vbook.fbsd.ru:/usr/obj/usr/src/sys/VBOOK
>        =20
>        =20
>        =20
>         >Description:
>        =20
>        =20
>         same for both:
>         graphics/gqview
>         graphics/gqview-devel
>        =20
>         % gqview
>         GQview 2.1.1, This is a beta release.
>         GTK Accessibility Module initialized
>         /libexec/ld-elf.so.1: /usr/local/lib/libgthread-2.0.so.0:
>         Undefined symbol "pthread_getschedparam"=20
>         %
>=20
> What version of gtk do you have installed? From ports or from
> marcuscom ports?

from ports.

>=20
>         similar report about gvim:
>        =20
>         http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=3D101458
>        =20
>         looks like linking problem
>        =20
>        =20
>         >How-To-Repeat:
>        =20
>        =20
>         just run gqview build on 7-CURRENT.
>        =20
>        =20
>         >Fix:
>        =20
>        =20
>         No fix, only workaround:
>         env LD_PRELOAD=3D/usr/lib/libc_r.so gqview
>        =20
>        =20
